source.title: Pattern separation in the dentate gyrus and CA3 of the hippocampus

confidence_rationale: Foundational Science paper; canonical primary evidence that distinct CA3 ensembles for the same physical location coexist. Strong direct support for MECH-269 dual-trace at the population level. Mapping caveat: the trigger biology routes via DG-computed input distance, not via internal anchor V_s; the substrate plan should acknowledge the trigger pathway differs from the architectural design.
failure_signatures: Biological routing of which CA3 ensemble fires depends on upstream input difference computed by DG, NOT on anchor-internal staleness/V_s. If MECH-272 routing decisions are driven solely by V_s, the substrate misses the input-distance-driven mechanism documented here.

Two separable mechanisms (DG rate re-coding for fine-grained discrimination; CA3 ensemble switching for coarse change). A substrate that uses only one of these (e.g. only anchor switching) will fail at the regime the other handles.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Two complementary mechanisms perform pattern separation in hippocampus: DG decorrelates by graded re-coding of the same active population (rate remapping), while CA3 recruits nonoverlapping ensembles when environmental change crosses some threshold (global remapping).
mapping.ree_translation: Direct empirical anchor for MECH-269 dual-trace preservation: distinct CA3 ensembles for the same place coexist as a population fact. MECH-272 routing has a biological precedent in the input-distance-driven gating from DG/EC. But the substrate plan currently routes via internal V_s on the anchor; biology routes via upstream input distance computed before the anchor is selected. These can be reconciled if V_s is treated as a learned proxy for input-distance, but the difference should be flagged.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The morphing paradigm is environmental geometry only -- non-spatial schema chunks may not show identical dual-regime behaviour. The recordings dissociate DG from CA3 but CA1 readout is inferred. Rodent only.
mapping.source_context: Single-unit recordings in DG granule cells and CA3 pyramidal cells of rats exploring parametrically morphed arenas (square -> octagonal -> circular).

confidence_components.notes: Very high source quality; mapping fidelity strong for the substrate claim (multiple CA3 ensembles coexist for same place) but moderate for the routing claim (biology routes via input-distance, plan routes via V_s).
